retrieved.title: The academic year
retrieved.text:
The academic year is divided into winter semester and summer semester. Each semester consists of a period during which courses are held and a lecuture-free period ("holidays").

- The University of Vienna is closed on [public holidays](https://www.wien.gv.at/amtshelfer/feiertage/).
- [Application and admission periods for degree programmes without an entrance exam procedure](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Application and admission period for degree programmes with an entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/deadlines-for-entrance-exam-procedures/)

retrieved.text: period usually equals the length of the standard period of the curriculum. The transfer to the new curriculum comes into effect automatically at the end of this period. Information about the transfer is available in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your student record sheet (u:space > Personal matters > My documents). Application for transfer: You have to apply for transfer to a new curriculum at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme, provided that the transition period has not ended yet and both curricula are offered simultaneously. Recognition of exams: If your curriuclum does not provide any regulation on the recognition, your exams will be automatically recognised from the old curriculum to the new one after the admission . Otherwise, you must apply for the recognition of exams. For more information and advice, contact the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) in charge of your curriculum. Please note: Previously, inform yourself about possible legal consequences at your StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). It is not possible to reverse the transfer! Related Links: - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - Translation Before you can apply for admission to a degree programme, you must have the required documents translated - unless they are issued in German or English. retrieved.text: students deregister, you might be able to move up the list and get a free place. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. - #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.

reranked.text: period usually equals the length of the standard period of the curriculum. The transfer to the new curriculum comes into effect automatically at the end of this period. Information about the transfer is available in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your student record sheet (u:space > Personal matters > My documents). Application for transfer: You have to apply for transfer to a new curriculum at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme, provided that the transition period has not ended yet and both curricula are offered simultaneously. Recognition of exams: If your curriuclum does not provide any regulation on the recognition, your exams will be automatically recognised from the old curriculum to the new one after the admission . Otherwise, you must apply for the recognition of exams. For more information and advice, contact the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) in charge of your curriculum. Please note: Previously, inform yourself about possible legal consequences at your StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS). It is not possible to reverse the transfer! Related Links: - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) - Translation Before you can apply for admission to a degree programme, you must have the required documents translated - unless they are issued in German or English. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. reranked.title: Application and admission periods
reranked.text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.
